methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Rain AI is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by Gordon Wilson, currently valued at $500M post-money with $146M total raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 3 same-stage, same-sector private comparables (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate) and an ancillary public semiconductor benchmark set of 3 tickers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two quantitative methods triangulate Rain AI's fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $500M last post-money valuation, implying $1.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 3 private comparables yields $2.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.4x across the semiconductor peer set) cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue data, typical for private-stage companies. 

The triangulated valuation range is $850M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.5B. The current $500M valuation sits below the stage-progression floor and well below peer median, suggesting either undervaluation relative to peer benchmarks or reflected risk premium specific to Rain AI's execution or market position. Key uncertainties include the revenue scale and trajectory, capital efficiency relative to peer cohorts, and the duration and terms of any subsequent funding round.
